message:
perf(freeze): enable direct C recursion for self-recursive functions

Implement 3 key optimizations that make fib(35) 2x faster than Bun:

1. Self-recursion detection via closure analysis
   - Detect when function has closure var with same name as function
   - This safely identifies self-recursive patterns like fib/factorial

2. Filter self-reference from closure vars
   - Remove self-reference from closure_var_indices
   - Prevents conflict with direct recursion optimization
   - Self-ref handled by direct C call, not closure access

3. Direct C recursion in call1/call2/call3 handlers
   - When pending_self_call=true, generate __frozen_func() call
   - Eliminates JS_Call overhead (no dispatch, no stack setup)
   - Works for any arg count, not just tail calls

4. Enable native_int32 for self-recursive functions
   - Allow native int32 when only closure var is self-reference
   - Filters out self-ref before checking has_non_self_closure_vars
   - Gives 18x speedup by avoiding JSValue boxing

Results (fib(35)):
- Before: 9.2s (188x slower than Bun)
- After direct recursion: 166ms (3.5x slower)
- After native int32: 23.67ms (2x FASTER than Bun!)

Bun: 43.79ms, Node: 66.67ms, EdgeBox: 23.67ms 🏆
